items.title: Search underway in Alice Springs for missing five-year-old girl

children.content: The girl was reported missing in the early hours of this morning when she could not be located at her Alice Springs home.

image.altText: Young girl in pink dress does peace sign with fingers

items.title: FOI documents show acting PM sought 'due diligence' on NT administrator

children.content: Months before a furore over his social media posts, documents show the federal government completed background checks on controversial NT Administrator David Connolly.

image.altText: A graphic shows a photo of David Connolly alongside a stack of paperwork.

items.title: What Anzac Day means to those who have served Australia

children.content: Australians taking part in Anzac Day commemorations share stories of sacrifice and bravery, and what today means to them.

items.title: Industry 'alert but not alarmed' as native weevil develops a taste for cotton

children.content: Farmers and researchers notice a rise in bicoloured weevils feeding on cotton crops in the Northern Territory.

image.altText: Masses of bicoloured weevils on a cotton flower in the NT.

items.title: Indigenous solar projects to tackle diesel shortage concerns and halve power bills

children.content: Two remote Indigenous communities have attracted a combined $11 million in funding for solutions to tackle their fuel security and cost-of-living crises.

image.altText: A photo showing a person walking towards a tractor carrying solar panels surround by fence and car outside the fence

items.title: Concerns community cohesion is 'going out the door' after Anzac booing

children.content: Jeers could be heard for more than a minute as Uncle Ray Minniecon spoke at yesterday's Anzac service in Sydney with similar scenes playing out in Melbourne and Perth.

image.altText: a group of young indigenous males march in redfern at the coloured diggers ceremony for anzac day 2026

items.title: Interim coach Foran makes history as Sea Eagles continue revival

children.content: Manly's revival under interim coach Kieran Foran has continued with a fourth straight win, as the Sea Eagles scored five tries in the second half to sink the Parramatta Eels. Follow live.

image.altText: Ben Trbojevic of the Sea Eagles and Will Penisini of the Eels compete for the ball

items.title: Dawson fights back tears after return match as GWS ace takes stunning mark

children.content: Adelaide skipper Jordan Dawson breaks down in tears after playing his first match since his brother's tragic death, as GWS high-flyer Leek Aleer shows off his tremendous athleticism against North Melbourne. Follow live.

image.altText: Jordan Dawson shares a heartfelt hug with Hugh McCluggage

items.title: Prince Harry's comments about the US may loom over Charles's visit

children.content: Little more than a week after Meghan and Harry's "quasi royal" tour in Australia, King Charles will be conducting the real thing in America, with much higher stakes.

items.title: Shark-attracting 25-tonne whale carcass to close Sydney beaches for days

children.content: Popular beaches along Sydney's Royal National Park will remain closed for days, as authorities face the immense task of moving a large whale carcass that is attracting sharks.

image.altText: A large deceased whale washed up at Era Point in the Royal National Park, Sydney

items.title: Trump cancels Pakistan trip for US envoys as peace hopes fade

children.content: Donald Trump cancels a trip to Pakistan by US representatives Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, with talks aimed at ending the two-month Iran war at a standstill as both Tehran and Washington show little willingness to soften their terms.

items.title: Married couple retire in their 90s after running small town's only hardware store for decades

children.content: The Pickles have been running the only hardware store in Junee for six decades, but now they are handing over the keys.

items.title: Inside Chernobyl is a toxic world where frogs have turned black

children.content: Nuclear meltdown in 1986 reshaped ecosystems and human lives. Four decades on the legacy of the Chernobyl disaster is still unfolding.

image.altText: Two brown eyes with yellow and black nuclear symbols painted around them

items.title: Economist turns guerilla groundskeeper to save heritage building

children.content: After he was forced to retire, Craig James couldn't ignore the knee-high grass and decaying paint job on an 1840s sandstone building down the road.

image.altText: a man with glasses smiles standing in front of a sandstone building

items.title: What Ringo did after the Beatles stunned the music world and he's still doing it 56 years later

children.content: Ringo's childhood love of classic country still influences the new music that The Beatles's drummer makes.

items.title: Love story: Bonding with my daughter was amazing, but it took time

children.content: Perinatal depression and anxiety meant I thought my daughter would be better off with a different mum. But with the right support, things got better.

items.title: Walter Mikac lost his wife and daughters at Port Arthur. Thirty years on, this is his message

children.content: On April 28, 1996, a gunman killed 35 people at Port Arthur. Walter Mikac's wife and two daughters were among them. Thirty years on, he says gun control is as important as ever.

image.altText: a bald man in a light blue t shirt

items.title: Wheelchair user calls for 'common sense' after using accessible zone without permit

children.content: Disability advocate and wheelchair user Shane Hryhorec is calling for Adelaide Airport to use a common sense approach after being threatened with a fine for using an accessible pick-up zone without a permit.

image.altText: A man in a wheelchair with his service dog on his lap

items.title: Tasmanian coastal community on bushfire watch again

children.content: A coastal community recently ravaged by a bushfire has again been on alert in Tasmania.

image.altText: Bushfire smoke plume seen from a backyard.

items.title: Thousands of Dutch families adopted the graves of people they never knew

children.content: In The Netherlands, a quiet commitment to honour the young soldiers who were buried thousands of kilometres from home has continued for 80 years.

image.altText: illustration of man and girl under red umberella looking at graves with dutch windmill and poppies

items.title: Sex workers speak out about being caught in the middle of PNG's HIV crisis

children.content: Workers have told the ABC about the battle to stay safe as the Pacific nation battles a rising number of infections.

items.title: Dingo destroyed after week of attacks in WA's north-west

children.content: A male dingo has been "humanely destroyed" after multiple attacks within days of each other at Western Australia's famous Karijini National Park.

image.altText: A dingo runs along a park fence, surrounded by yellow and green scrub.

items.title: Act before commodities boom leaves us bust, urges former Treasury boss

children.content: Former Treasury secretary Ken Henry says the under-taxed resources sector has hollowed out Australia's economy, and raising more revenue from the sale of LNG resources was in the national interest.

image.altText: A man in a suit and white shirt stares into the distance

items.title: This plant is the only known wild specimen in the world, found on just 300m of roadside

children.content: Only one known single spyridium plant is left in the wild, in crushed limestone on the side of a road. But scientists are hunting for more.

image.altText: The small fuzzy white flower of the Woods Well Spyridium

items.title: Social licence test shows why Labor acts on NDIS but not gas

children.content: Labor's radical transformation of the NDIS as it resists a growing chorus of support for a new gas tax exposes the limit of its willingness to act when given a social licence.

image.altText: The prime minister looks at journalists.

items.title: In the aftermath of complex grief there can be post-traumatic growth

children.content: It's normal to feel distress when you go through trauma. But some people also experience personal growth to make positive changes in their lives.

gallery.altText: Image of Blake Johnston surfing a wave wearing a cowboy hat.

thumbnail.altText: Image of Blake Johnston surfing a wave wearing a cowboy hat.

items.title: Two out of three stolen cars are left with 'some form of hazard' in them

children.content: 'Opportunistic' car thieves are leaving vehicles littered with syringes, weapons and even traps, and in 2025, 77 per cent of vehicles inspected by one contractor had signs of methylamphetamine used at levels harmful to health.

image.altText: A man in a blue shirt sitting in front of a blue ute in his driveway.

items.title: Victoria offers 20pc car rego discount in cost-of-living measure

children.content: Motorists will have two months to claim a 20 per cent rebate on car registration as the government attempts to counter price rises at the petrol pump.

image.altText: A licence plate

items.title: Second population of ancient, 'sterile' plant discovered

children.content: A second population of an ancient tree, King's lomatia, has been found in the south-west of Tasmania. A scientist says the precise location must be kept secret, or risk being 'killed by love'.

items.title: Police shoot at car amid chaotic night across Melbourne

children.content: Police are investigating multiple incidents overnight including a drive-by shooting, suspected firebombing and an allegedly violent attack on a tram.

items.title: 'Waiting for Armageddon': Nimbin preppers ready if world goes up in smoke

children.content: It may not be the end of the world, but these Australians are finding creative ways to beat the cost-of-living crisis and improve their food security in troubled times.

children.content: North West Island is one of five national parks where you can holiday in exchange for campground hosting duties.

items.title: Housing developer Assemble slashes number of promised affordable homes

children.content: Victorian planning authorities will allow Assemble to significantly reduce the number of affordable homes offered at large developments in Melbourne's north by more than half.

items.title: Stilt stunt for the ages as one man attempts half-marathon world record

children.content: Santiago Vergara, 33, hopes to have nabbed a Guinness World Record after running the Ballarat half-marathon on stilts in a time of 2 hours and 34 minutes.
